name: Help Scout API
what_it_does: Help Scout REST API — programmatic access to conversations, mailboxes, customers, and teams in the email-first customer support platform, enabling agents to automate support workflows, respond to conversations, and sync customer data.
use_cases: Agents auto-triaging incoming support conversations by analyzing content and assigning to appropriate team queues
AI-powered reply drafting — agents generating draft responses to common support questions for human agent review
Customer data enrichment — agents fetching customer conversation history from Help Scout to inform sales or onboarding workflows
SLA monitoring — agents querying conversation response times to identify at-risk conversations before SLA breach
Support ticket sync — agents keeping Help Scout conversations in sync with Jira, Linear, or GitHub Issues for bug tracking
not_for: Complex multi-channel support — Zendesk or Freshdesk handle phone, chat, and social alongside email more comprehensively
Enterprise ITSM workflows — Help Scout is optimized for customer support, not IT service management
Live chat as primary channel — Help Scout's chat (Beacon) is secondary; use Intercom or Crisp for chat-first support
best_when: You need to automate email-first customer support workflows for a team that values simplicity and a human-feeling inbox over complex ticketing systems.
avoid_when: You need complex ticket workflows, SLAs, or multi-channel support — Zendesk or Freshdesk have more sophisticated automation capabilities.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials (app ID + secret) for server-to-server integration. OAuth 2.0 authorization code for user-level access. Access tokens expire after 48 hours — implement refresh. Scopes control mailbox and customer access.

pricing.notes: 15-day free trial. API access included in all plans. Mailbox and user limits vary by plan. Annual billing discounts available. No per-API-call pricing.

performance.rate_limits: 200 req/min per access token; webhook delivery within seconds; bulk operations rate-limited separately

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-documented API with clear examples. OAuth flow documented thoroughly. Error responses include error code and message. Webhook payloads well-documented. Rate limit headers included in responses.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Conversation creation is not idempotent — duplicate conversations can be created. Replies are not idempotent. Customer creation by email is idempotent — returns existing customer if email matches.
known_agent_gotchas: Access tokens expire after 48 hours — agents must implement token refresh using refresh token before expiry
Conversation vs Thread distinction — conversations are the container; threads are individual messages within
Help Scout Beacon (chat) uses a different API than the Mailbox API — separate docs and endpoints
Assigning conversations requires knowing mailbox ID and user ID — pre-fetch these before assignment operations
Webhook signature verification uses HMAC-SHA1 (not SHA-256) — verify against X-HelpScout-Signature header

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. HIPAA BAA available. GDPR compliant with EU data residency. OAuth 2.0 with scopes. US and EU hosting options. Access tokens expire in 48 hours — short-lived. Enterprise security review available.
